inlineContent.text: Be the hero to change that by watching the video or reading the transcript on WWDCIndex and providing notes! It’s super easy:

abstract.text: Auto Layout is a powerful constraint-based layout engine that can drive complex and dynamic interfaces on both iOS and OS X.   Learn about the new UIStackView, now your first stop for interfaces built with Auto Layout. See how to build self-sizing views using Auto Layout, and gain critical insights into the inner workings of Auto Layout.

Get details about new features of Auto Layout in Xcode, Cocoa and Cocoa Touch. Explore the new NSGridView, allowing your Cocoa app to easily build grid interfaces. Dive deeper into new tools that enable you to quickly diagnose layout issues in your interface. Learn about the new features in Xcode to quickly build adaptive interfaces.

Do you have an idea for an app that you think would be useful to others, but you don’t know how to make it a reality? You’re not alone! Lots of people are interested in app development, but they aren’t sure how to turn their idea to code and then into a finished app. From first steps to Xcode, we’ll explain the building blocks you need to turn your iOS app idea into reality.

doc://WWDCNotes/documentation/WWDCNotes/.title: I Have This Idea For An App…
Choosing the Right Cocoa Container View

abstract.text: AppKit offers numerous ways to easily present your data. Join our framework engineers for a guided tour of versatile standard view classes you can put to work in your own macOS apps.  Hear about NSStackView, NSTableView, NSCollectionView, and other container views in AppKit.  Explore the interesting features and benefits of each, and examine real-world use cases to help you choose the most suitable building blocks for your apps’ user interfaces.
Xcode 7, Swift 2.0, iOS 9, macOS 10.11 (El Capitan), watchOS 2.
Session notes shared by the community for the community.

Get a glimpse inside to find out what happens when you add a constraint! Dive into the internals of Auto Layout to develop intuition for how your code affects what happens under the hood. Learn how to measure and refine your approach to Auto Layout and see how its improved performance in iOS 12 will speed up your app.
